lines.english: Aasaa, Fifth Mehl:
The mortal revels in joy, in the vigor of youth;
but without the Name, he mingles with dust. ||1||
Inwardly and outwardly, He has shown me the One Lord. ||4||3||54||
He may wear ear-rings and fine clothes,
and have a comfortable bed, and his mind may be so proud. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਬਿਨਾ ਲੇ ਧਰਨਿ ਗਡਲੀਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: har bhagat binaa le dharan gaddaleea |2|
lines.english: but without devotional worship to the Lord, he is buried beneath the dirt. ||2||
He may have elephants to ride, and golden umbrellas over his head;
Deluded by Maya, the mortal is led into sin and corruption.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਸ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਭਿ ਸੁਆਦ ਫਿਕਰੀਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: har ras bin sabh suaad fikareea |3|
lines.english: but without the sublime essence of the Lord, all tastes are tasteless. ||3||
He may enjoy many women, of exquisite beauty;
There is a garden, in which so many plants have grown.
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl:
Nanak seeks the Sanctuary of God, the All-powerful, Compassionate Lord. ||4||4||55||
Consider this, O wise one,
They bear the Ambrosial Nectar of the Naam as their fruit. ||1||
by which you may attain the state of Nirvaanaa.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਿ ਪਾਸਿ ਬਿਖੂਆ ਕੇ ਕੁੰਟਾ ਬੀਚਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਹੈ ਭਾਈ ਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aas paas bikhooaa ke kunttaa beech amrit hai bhaaee re |1| rahaau |
lines.english: All around this garden are pools of poison, but within it is the Ambrosial Nectar, O Siblings of Destiny. ||1||Pause||
They all bear fruit - none is without fruit. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਬਰਿ ਕਰਤੁ ਹੈ ਪਾਤ ਪਤ ਡਾਲੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: khabar karat hai paat pat ddaalee |2|
lines.english: He takes care of every leaf and branch. ||2||
There is only one gardener who tends it.
He brings all sorts of plants and plants them there.
- O Nanak, such a servant crosses over the ocean of Maya. ||4||5||56||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਫਲੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: amrit fal naam jin gur te paaeaa |
lines.english: One who receives the Ambrosial Fruit of the Naam from the Guru
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl:
I attain Yoga, singing the Kirtan of Your Praises. ||1||
The pleasures of royalty are derived from Your Name.
Understanding the Command of the Lord's Will, I revel in pleasure and joy.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭ੍ਰਮ ਕੇ ਪਰਦੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਖੋਲੑੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: bhram ke parade satigur kholae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The True Guru has removed the veil of doubt. ||1||Pause||
All comforts are obtained in Your Shelter.
Serving the True Guru, I obtain the supreme state of Nirvaanaa. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਤੂੰ ਜਾਤਾ ਸੋ ਗਿਰਸਤ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: jin toon jaataa so girasat udaasee paravaan |
lines.english: One who recognizes You is recognized as a householder, and as a renunciate.
Imbued with the Naam, the Name of the Lord, he dwells in Nirvaanaa. ||3||
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl:
One who has obtained the treasure of the Naam

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਨਤਿ ਨਾਨਕ ਤਾ ਕਾ ਪੂਰ ਖਜਾਨਾ ॥੪॥੬॥੫੭॥
lines.translit: bhanat naanak taa kaa poor khajaanaa |4|6|57|
lines.english: - prays Nanak, his treasure-house is filled to overflowing. ||4||6||57||
If I ask the Pandits, I find them tainted by Maya. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰਥਿ ਜਾਉ ਤ ਹਉ ਹਉ ਕਰਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: teerath jaau ta hau hau karate |
lines.english: Journeying to sacred shrines of pilgrimage, I see the mortals acting in ego.
Show me that place, O friend,
The Shaastras and the Vedas speak of sin and virtue;
they say that mortals are reincarnated into heaven and hell, over and over again. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕੈ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਨੁ ਨੀਤਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jaa kai har har keeratan neetaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: where the Kirtan of the Lord's Praises are forever sung. ||1||Pause||
In the householder's life, there is anxiety, and in the life of the renunciate, there is egotism.
Performing religious rituals, the soul is entangled. ||3||
In the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy, sing the Kirtan of the Lord's Praises.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਮਨੁ ਵਸਿ ਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: prabh kirapaa te man vas aaeaa |
lines.english: By God's Grace, the mind is brought under control;
O Nanak, the Gurmukh crosses over the ocean of Maya. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰ ਮਹਿ ਸੂਖ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਫੁਨਿ ਸੂਖਾ ॥
lines.translit: ghar meh sookh baahar fun sookhaa |
lines.english: Within my home there is peace, and outwardly there is peace as well.
Aasaa, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਅਸਥਾਨੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਤੇ ਪਾਈਐ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ਦੂਜਾ ॥੭॥੫੮॥
lines.translit: eihu asathaan guroo te paaeeai |1| rahaau doojaa |7|58|
lines.english: This place is found through the Guru. ||1||Second Pause||7||58||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲ ਸੂਖ ਜਾਂ ਤੂੰ ਚਿਤਿ ਆਂਵੈਂ ॥
lines.translit: sagal sookh jaan toon chit aanvain |
lines.english: There is total peace, when You come into my mind.
Remembering the Lord in meditation, all pains are erased. ||1||
